Operation 'listCustomers' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details. Original description is too minimal and lacks context about pagination, return structure, and filtering capabilities. Enhanced version clarifies the paginated nature of the response, specifies the return type (CustomerList), and mentions pagination support to help AI agents understand how to retrieve customer data effectively.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of all customers created in your Mollie account. Returns a CustomerList object containing customer records with their details. Supports pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
data.reasoning: Original description is too minimal and lacks context about pagination, return structure, and filtering capabilities. Enhanced version clarifies the paginated nature of the response, specifies the return type (CustomerList), and mentions pagination support to help AI agents understand how to retrieve customer data effectively.

message: Operation 'listMethods' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details. Summary is adequate, but description lacks specificity about what payment methods are available and the structure of the response. Enhanced description clarifies the return type (MethodList), provides examples of supported payment methods from the API context, and mentions filtering/pagination capabilities to help AI agents understand how to retrieve and filter payment method information.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a list of all available payment methods supported by Mollie. Returns a MethodList object containing payment method details including supported payment types (iDEAL, credit cards, SOFORT, Bancontact, Giftcards, etc.). Supports filtering and pagination parameters to customize results.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate, but description lacks specificity about what payment methods are available and the structure of the response. Enhanced description clarifies the return type (MethodList), provides examples of supported payment methods from the API context, and mentions filtering/pagination capabilities to help AI agents understand how to retrieve and filter payment method information.

message: Operation 'createPayment' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original summary is too generic. Description lacks clarity about what inputs are expected (amount, currency, etc.), what is returned (payment ID, checkout URL), and the initial payment state. Added specifics about request parameters, response structure, and side effects for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description: To accept a payment from a shopper, you need to create a payment object that will contain all the necessary payment information.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new payment object to initiate a transaction from a shopper. Accepts payment details including amount, currency, description, and payment method preferences. Returns a Payment object with a unique payment ID and checkout URL for the shopper to complete the transaction. The payment status will initially be 'open' until the shopper completes the payment flow.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic. Description lacks clarity about what inputs are expected (amount, currency, etc.), what is returned (payment ID, checkout URL), and the initial payment state. Added specifics about request parameters, response structure, and side effects for better AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'listPayments' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Summary is too vague and doesn't indicate pagination. Description lacks information about available parameters (filtering, pagination), response structure (PaymentList with pagination), and use cases. Enhanced to clarify pagination support and response format for AI agents.

data.current_description: Retrieve all payments created with the current website profile.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of all payments created with the current website profile. Supports filtering and pagination parameters. Returns a PaymentList object containing an array of Payment objects and pagination metadata. Useful for reconciliation, reporting, and payment status tracking.
data.reasoning: Summary is too vague and doesn't indicate pagination. Description lacks information about available parameters (filtering, pagination), response structure (PaymentList with pagination), and use cases. Enhanced to clarify pagination support and response format for AI agents.

message: Operation 'getPayment' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Summary uses imprecise terminology ('payment token' should be 'payment ID'). Description lacks information about what fields are returned and practical use cases. Updated to clarify the identifier type and provide context about response contents and typical usage patterns.

data.current_description: Retrieve a single payment object by its payment token.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a single Payment object by its unique payment ID. Returns complete payment details including status, amount, method, customer information, and timestamps. Use this to check payment status, retrieve transaction details, or verify payment completion.
data.reasoning: Summary uses imprecise terminology ('payment token' should be 'payment ID'). Description lacks information about what fields are returned and practical use cases. Updated to clarify the identifier type and provide context about response contents and typical usage patterns.

message: Operation 'cancelPayment' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is too generic. Description is vague about preconditions (payment must be 'open'), doesn't specify what is returned, and lacks clarity about the side effect (payment becomes uncompleted). Enhanced to clarify status requirements, response format, and consequences of cancellation for AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description: Some payment methods are cancellable for an amount of time, usually until the next business day.
data.description_suggestion: Cancels an open payment if the payment method supports cancellation. Cancellation is typically available until the next business day depending on the payment method. Only payments with 'open' status can be cancelled. Returns the updated Payment object with status changed to 'cancelled'. After cancellation, the payment cannot be completed by the shopper.
data.reasoning: Summary is too generic. Description is vague about preconditions (payment must be 'open'), doesn't specify what is returned, and lacks clarity about the side effect (payment becomes uncompleted). Enhanced to clarify status requirements, response format, and consequences of cancellation for AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'createRefund' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is too vague. Description lacks critical details about input requirements (amount parameter), preconditions (payment status must be 'paid'), response structure (Refund object), and important constraints (multiple refunds, total limit). Added specifics to help AI agents determine when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description: Most payment methods support refunds. A payment can be partially or fully refunded.
data.summary_suggestion: Create a refund for a payment
data.description_suggestion: Creates a refund for a completed payment. Supports both full and partial refunds - specify the refund amount in the request body. The payment must have a 'paid' status to be refunded. Returns a Refund object with a unique refund ID and status. The refund will be processed according to the payment method's refund timeline. Multiple refunds can be created for a single payment as long as the total doesn't exceed the original amount.
data.reasoning: Summary is too vague. Description lacks critical details about input requirements (amount parameter), preconditions (payment status must be 'paid'), response structure (Refund object), and important constraints (multiple refunds, total limit). Added specifics to help AI agents determine when and how to use this operation.

message: Operation 'createCustomer' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Summary is too generic. Description is vague about inputs (what customer details are accepted), outputs (what is returned), and practical use cases. Enhanced to clarify request parameters, response structure (Customer object with ID), and the relationship to subscriptions and recurring payments for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: Creates a simple minimal representation of a customer in the Mollie API to use for the Mollie Checkout and Recurring features.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a customer profile in the Mollie API to enable recurring payments and Mollie Checkout functionality. Accepts customer details such as name, email, and locale. Returns a Customer object with a unique customer ID that can be used for creating subscriptions, recurring payments, and storing payment methods. Customer profiles are required for subscription management.
data.reasoning: Summary is too generic. Description is vague about inputs (what customer details are accepted), outputs (what is returned), and practical use cases. Enhanced to clarify request parameters, response structure (Customer object with ID), and the relationship to subscriptions and recurring payments for better AI agent understanding.
